_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
specialty: General Surgery / Pediatric Surgery / Vascular Surgery
clinical_indications: To relieve ischemia when the fingers, toes, ears, or nose are exposed to cold (Raynaud's phenomenon)
To help treat Reflex Sympathetic Dystrophy (RSD), which sometimes develops after an injury
To effectively treat excessive sweating (Hyperhidrosis) of the palms, armpits, or face
To relieve rest pain in Thromboangitis obliterans (TAO)
For cutaneous ulcer in TAO patients (if direct arterial surgery is not possible)

preauth.label: Clinical Notes with Indications and planned line of treatment

ppd_preauth.q: Were the patient's clinical history/investigations indicative of the Procedure?
Whether the investigation reports confirm the diagnosis?

common_queries: Patient's clinical history or investigations not indicative of the procedure
Investigation reports do not confirm the diagnosis
Clinical notes with indications and planned line of treatment not submitted or incomplete
Clinical photograph not submitted or unclear
Doppler Ultrasound Blood Flow report not submitted
Detailed Indoor Case Papers (ICPs) not submitted or incomplete

clinical_key_pointers: Sympathectomy is a surgical procedure that destroys nerves in the sympathetic nervous system.
The procedure increases blood flow and decreases long-term pain in certain diseases that cause narrowed blood vessels.
It can also be used to reduce excessive sweating.
This surgical procedure cuts or destroys the sympathetic ganglia, a collection of nerve cell bodies in clusters along the thoracic or lumbar spinal cord.
additional_information.pre_op_preparation: A reversible block of the affected nerve cell (ganglion) determines if sympathectomy is needed.
